data.narrative:
Newspace Research is a Series B-stage defense and security company founded by Sameer Joshi that has raised $73.2M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 peers across Defense & Security, including 8 private companies and 2 public names (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS). Key private comparables include Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns, all at Series B stage.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement to the $73M raised proxy, yielding $139M. Peer-median benchmarking across six same-sector, same-stage companies produces a $1.2B implied valuation; the peer set ranges from $1.0B (Castelion, Defense Unicorns) to $1.5B (Vannevar Labs, Dream Security), with a median of $1.2B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $118M to $1.4B, with a median of $670M. The substantial spread reflects tension between stage-based progression (which anchors toward the low end) and peer multiples (which anchor toward the high end). Key uncertainties include Newspace's relative product maturity, customer traction, and capital efficiency versus the named peer set. Additional clarity on revenue scale and growth rate would materially narrow this range.
